project_name: Developer Workflow Persona Drift Benchmark Against FileGram

decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as a differentiated developer-workflow persona-drift benchmark; future work can calibrate against official FileGram judge labels if those labels become available.
decision.stop_reason: The branch now has a synthetic developer drift benchmark, an apples-to-apples public FileGram bridge, and a local git/test telemetry realism audit that clears the branch-specific kill condition.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as a differentiated developer-workflow persona-drift benchmark; future work can calibrate against official FileGram judge labels if those labels become available.
run_notes_tail: - Added `scripts/developer_realism_audit.py`, a deterministic local telemetry audit over actual git checkouts.
- Updated `README.md` with the new reproduction command and audit outputs.
- Dimension support against the synthetic benchmark:
- weak: none under this local-only audit.
- The branch now has three evidence layers: a developer-workflow drift benchmark, a shared FileGram replay bridge, and a realism audit over actual local developer repositories.

- The audit supports the kill-condition escape: the benchmark dimensions are not just generic file naming/directory preferences; most map to real codebase, build, test, CI, commit, branch, and review observables.
- Remaining scientific caveat: hosted issue/PR labels or official FileGram judge labels would still improve absolute calibration, especially for PR-style and debug-loop semantics.
